Explanation: Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed using select(column1, column2, ...).from(getTable()).

Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the table for every record in the stream, which can be inefficient. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using select(column1, column2, ...).from(getTable()).

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%value%') for searching first names forces a full table scan and cannot use indexes efficiently. Consider using a full-text search index or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch for text searching.

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%value%') for searching last names forces a full table scan and cannot use indexes efficiently. Consider using a full-text search index or a dedicated search engine like Elasticsearch for text searching.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the Person table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., .select(personTab.PERSON_ID, personTab.FIRST_NAME, personTab.LAST_NAME, ...).
